steps.title: Condition for no solution: parallel lines
steps.content: A system has no solution when the two lines are parallel — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts.

steps.title: Find the choice with slope 4 and a different y-intercept
steps.content: The given equation $y=4x+12$ is $-4x+y=12$ in standard form. Choice A (‘$-4x+y=12$’) is the same equation, so it gives infinitely many solutions. Choice B, $-4x+y=15$, has the same $x$- and $y$-coefficients but a different constant, so it is parallel and distinct. The other choices have slope $8$, so they intersect the given line once. That confirms B is correct.
